headline: Romania wakes up to its vulnerability to Russian drones: 'We are theoretically in a country at peace'
description: For the first time since Russia invaded Ukraine, a Russian drone struck a residential building in Romania on May 29, injuring two people. The incident in the city of Galati, near the Ukrainian border, has exposed the civil protection system's flaws, the poor condition of shelters and the lack of public information.

headline: 'I am available,' investment banker Matthieu Pigasse tells the left ahead of 2027 election
Under pressure, Trump administration drops controversial fund for Capitol rioters
Denis Mukwege, Nobel Peace Prize laureate: 'This Ebola outbreak could become the deadliest ever'
description: The Ebola virus is spreading across the ruins left by war in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, warns the doctor, urging all the warring parties to accept an immediate ceasefire in order to contain the outbreak.

headline: Why consumer electronics prices are surging
description: Component prices have surged in recent months, driven by both the race to develop artificial intelligence and by the war in Iran. This trend is now affecting consumer electronics, with no respite expected in the short term.

headline: Les Nuits de Fourvière in Lyon unveils a vibrant program to celebrate the festival's 80th anniversary
description: Running through July 25, the festival features 60 open-air shows, concerts, performances and masked balls, including 'To See the Stars Again' by the Australian contemporary circus troupe Circa.

headline: The 'palace' paradox: Most of France's top luxury hotels are owned by foreigners
description: The government's latest 'Palaces de France' list was revealed on June 2, with six new additions and four removals. The label aims to showcase this flagship sector of French expertise, even though the establishments' owners are rarely French.

The 20 French cities where the country's wealthiest live
description: The city of Neuilly-sur-Seine outside Paris has the highest proportion of very wealthy households in France, according to a study by the Inequality Observatory published on Tuesday. The ranking is dominated by the western suburbs of Paris and the areas surrounding Geneva.

headline: Putin faces a series of setbacks, fueling discontent in Russia and raising doubts about his strategy
description: While the Russian president claims that victory in Ukraine is within reach, Kyiv is stepping up drone strikes deep inside Russian territory. These military setbacks compound the country's mounting economic difficulties.

headline: Trump weakens Netanyahu by forcing him to delay attack on Beirut
description: The US president ordered Israel not to attack the Lebanese capital. As with Gaza or Iran, only Donald Trump can restrain Israel's military force. This has caused frustration within the country's governing coalition, further weakening Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
